detail.change_summary: OpenAI changed eligible API container sessions to per-minute billing with a shorter minimum billing window while keeping the underlying per-minute rate unchanged.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official changelog reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.basis: official OpenAI API changelog entry dated 2026-06-02
estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: eligible shorter container sessions avoid the former full 20-minute minimum and instead use a 5-minute minimum

impacts.recommended_action: Revisit cost models for OpenAI API workflows that create short-lived eligible container sessions, and keep current built-in tool pricing docs as the source for exact rates.

limitations: APW records the public changelog billing mechanism change only; exact eligibility, tool coverage, account rollout, and current per-minute rates remain governed by OpenAI's pricing documentation.
summary: OpenAI changed eligible API container sessions from full-session minimum billing to per-minute billing with a shorter minimum, reducing effective cost for shorter eligible sessions.
title: OpenAI Changed Eligible Container Sessions To Per-Minute Billing

detail.change_summary: OpenAI announced that OpenAI frontier models and Codex are generally available on AWS, including Amazon Bedrock and AWS governance channels.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official announcement reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.
evidence_refs.selector: OpenAI on AWS general availability announcement

evidence_refs.license_note: Official API changelog reviewed for corroborating factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

impacts.recommended_action: Evaluate whether AWS-native procurement, IAM, network controls, and Bedrock governance change the preferred route for OpenAI model or Codex adoption.
Compare Codex feature availability and data-governance controls across ChatGPT sign-in, OpenAI API key, and Amazon Bedrock API key configurations.
For regulated workloads, verify region availability, feature parity, and account approvals before migrating provider access paths.

limitations: APW records availability and channel facts only; it does not assert feature parity, account eligibility, or exact AWS regional coverage beyond the official announcement.
summary: OpenAI announced general availability of OpenAI frontier models and Codex on AWS, adding an AWS procurement, governance, and deployment channel for affected customers.
title: OpenAI Frontier Models And Codex Became Available On AWS
